seoTitle: Bentley vs Berkshire Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bentley and Berkshire Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Bentley vs Berkshire Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bentley and Berkshire Valley.
heading: Bentley vs Berkshire Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bentley, Western Australia and Berkshire Valley, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Berkshire Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Bentley (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bentley or Berkshire Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bentley has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Berkshire Valley scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bentley scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Berkshire Valley scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bentley or Berkshire Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Berkshire Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
